name: Mee Krob (Sweet Thai Noodles)
recipeIngredient: 1 bunch Green onions (whites only), cut into thin strips
8 oz Tofu, firm or extra-firm, cubed
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small saucepan, combine water, sugar, vinegar, and salt. Bring to a gentle boil and cook for about ten minutes until it forms a thin syrup. Set aside.
2. Rinse dried shrimp under running water. Drain in sieve. Remove stems from chilies but keep seeds. Slice green onions and chilies into thin strips. Reserve.
3. Cut tofu into 1/4-inch cubes. Beat eggs lightly until well mixed. Strain through fine sieve. Reserve.
4. Heat oil in wok to 400°F (204°C). Drain tofu on paper towels after frying until firm and light golden, not dry or hard. Remove tofu from oil and drain on towels.
5. Fry noodles a handful at a time in hot oil. They should puff up and turn brown in about ten seconds. If they don't expand immediately, the oil isn't hot enough. If they burn quickly, reduce heat. Drain noodles on paper towels. Save some oil for later use.
6. Dribble beaten eggs over hot oil in wok to form narrow strands. Stretch egg from bowl over oil and let it flow in a thin stream while moving hand in circular motion. Repeat until surface of oil covered with thin net of egg. When strands are set and light golden, flip them carefully and brown the other side. Drain on towels.
7. Dry shrimp thoroughly. Fry until just crisp and light golden, about three minutes. Remove from oil and drain on towels.
8. Clean wok and heat sugar syrup almost to boiling without letting it boil. Add noodles, egg strands, tofu, and shrimp. Cook until syrup is absorbed and noodles are cooked through. Repeat with remaining ingredients.
9. Garnish Mee Krob with green onion whites and chilies. Serve immediately or hold at room temperature for up to two hours.
